name: Schema migration that splits a column while preserving data
setup: mkdir -p /tmp/eval_migrate && rm -f /tmp/eval_migrate/users.db && python3 -c "import sqlite3; c = sqlite3.connect('/tmp/eval_migrate/users.db'); c.execute('CREATE TABLE users(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, full_name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT)'); c.executemany('INSERT INTO users(full_name, email) VALUES (?, ?)', [('Alice Smith', 'a@x'), ('Bob Jones', 'b@x'), ('Carol Anne Davis', 'c@x'), ('Dan', 'd@x'), ('Eve Brown', 'e@x')]); c.commit(); c.close()"
task: Create /tmp/eval_migrate/migrate.py with a function migrate(db_path) that performs a forward schema migration on a SQLite database. The existing users table has columns (id, full_name, email). After migration the table must have (id, first_name, last_name, email) where: first_name = the part before the first space in full_name; last_name = everything after the first space (joined back with spaces) or empty string if there is none. ALL existing rows must survive with their id and email unchanged. The full_name column must no longer exist. The migration must be safe to run inside a transaction (commit at the end, rollback on exception). Use only the sqlite3 standard library.

validation.script: import importlib.util, sqlite3; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_migrate/migrate.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); mod.migrate('/tmp/eval_migrate/users.db'); c = sqlite3.connect('/tmp/eval_migrate/users.db'); cols = [r[1] for r in c.execute('PRAGMA table_info(users)').fetchall()]; assert set(cols) == {'id', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'email'}, f'columns: {cols}'; rows = c.execute('SELECT id, first_name, last_name, email FROM users ORDER BY id').fetchall(); expected = [(1, 'Alice', 'Smith', 'a@x'), (2, 'Bob', 'Jones', 'b@x'), (3, 'Carol', 'Anne Davis', 'c@x'), (4, 'Dan', '', 'd@x'), (5, 'Eve', 'Brown', 'e@x')]; assert rows == expected, f'rows mismatch:\n  got      {rows}\n  expected {expected}'; print('OK')
